rationale: The largest failure class is SQLWriter wrong_result on derived/qualitative answers, multi-part questions, and superlative-over-metric semantics; I add a strategy section plus two targeted memory pitfalls for those. I reinforce the no-clarification-stall rule at the orchestrator level so runs always produce executable SQL, and add a schema_linker memory for coded-enum/domain-literal confirmation.

prompt_patches.new_text: For every question: (1) List each interrogative clause ('which', 'how many', 'what', 'was it') and produce exactly one output column per clause — a multi-part question needs multiple columns. (2) When the question wants a qualitative/boolean verdict (e.g. 'was it well-finished', 'is it closed', 'single-bonded?'), do NOT return the raw underlying column; translate it to the implied label via CASE (e.g. ClosedDate IS NULL -> not closed). (3) For superlatives, identify the exact METRIC NOUN and rank on THAT column, not a similarly-named attribute ('highest potential score for crossing' ranks by potential among crossing-related rows). (4) Compute MAX/MIN over the filtered subset the question restricts to, and per-entity (GROUP BY the entity) rather than globally, unless the question is explicitly global. (5) 'average price per single item' = AVG(Price/Amount) per row (CAST AS REAL), not SUM(Price)/SUM(Amount). Verify every clause maps to a column before handoff. Never emit a clarifying question as your answer — state one assumption line and produce executable SQL.

This is a BIRD-style benchmark: the correct output is a single executable SQL query, not a dialogue. No agent may terminate a run by asking the user a clarifying question. If interpretation is ambiguous, instruct the responsible agent to adopt the most common/literal reading, state the assumption in one line, and proceed. Require that SchemaLinker and SQLWriter each hand off a concrete executable candidate query before control reaches the Validator; a handoff without runnable SQL is invalid and must be sent back. Ensure multi-part questions are decomposed so every interrogative clause maps to an output column.

memory_upserts.text: Two recurring wrong_result traps: (a) qualitative/boolean questions ('well-finished','closed','all single bonds') must map the raw column to the implied label via CASE (NULL ClosedDate -> not closed; bond_type='-' for all bonds -> single-bonded), never return the raw column. (b) Superlatives: rank on the exact metric noun and per-entity within the filtered subset, not the wrong attribute or a global MAX.

For multi-part questions, enumerate each sub-question and emit one output column per sub-question in asked order. Example: 'top spender, avg price per item, and currency' -> SELECT entity, AVG(Price/Amount) unit_price, currency. Prefer AVG(Price/Amount) for 'average price per single item', not SUM(Price)/SUM(Amount).

When a WHERE literal is a domain term, sample_rows the candidate column to confirm stored encoding before committing. card_games: colors/colorIdentity use single-letter codes (B=black,W=white,U=blue,R=red,G=green); card type filters belong on 'type'/'types', not 'originalType'. Pass the exact confirmed literal to the writer.
